candidates.name: Agent-Build Course
candidates.summary: A structured course that walks builders through agentic design patterns — reflection, tool use, planning, and multi-agent collaboration — using code labs so they graduate able to build and ship a working agent.
mechanics: Teach the four foundational agentic design patterns: reflection, tool use, planning, multi-agent
Each pattern is paired with a runnable code example in a real framework (LangGraph, CrewAI, smolagents, or vendor SDK)
Capstone: build one working agent that uses at least two patterns against a real task
Assessment either via graded assignments or benchmark leaderboard (e.g. GAIA)
proof: Participant ships one deployed agent that uses tool-call and at least one other pattern
Earns a certificate of completion (Hugging Face, DeepLearning.AI, IBM Coursera, Microsoft Learn)

known_uses.name: Agentic AI
known_uses.quote: build agentic design patterns: reflection, tool use, planning, and multi-agent workflows
known_uses.note: Taught by Andrew Ng; 31 video lessons, 7 code examples, vendor-neutral Python; released 2025

known_uses.name: AI Agents in LangGraph
known_uses.quote: Build an agent from scratch, and understand the division of tasks between the LLM and the code around the LLM.
known_uses.note: Taught by Harrison Chase (LangChain CEO) and Rotem Weiss (Tavily CEO); intermediate, 9 lessons

known_uses.name: Hugging Face AI Agents Course
known_uses.quote: Learn to use established AI Agent libraries such as smolagents, LlamaIndex, and LangGraph.
known_uses.note: Free; certificate of completion; final project benchmarked on GAIA; 3-4 hrs/week over 4 units

known_uses.name: Design, Develop, and Deploy Multi-Agent Systems with CrewAI
known_uses.quote: Bridge that gap by turning prototypes like an automated code reviewer, a meeting co-pilot, and a deep researcher into production-ready systems.
known_uses.note: Taught by João Moura (CrewAI CEO); 4 modules covering planning, memory, guardrails, MCP, CI/CD

known_uses.name: IBM RAG and Agentic AI Professional Certificate
known_uses.quote: equips software engineers with advanced generative AI skills, focusing on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), autonomous AI agents, LangChain framework implementations, and production-grade AI system deployment
known_uses.note: 9-course professional certificate; 3-month timeline; frameworks include LangGraph, CrewAI, AG2, BeeAI, MCP

sources.quote: build agentic design patterns: reflection, tool use, planning, and multi-agent workflows

sources.title: AI Agents Course — Hugging Face
sources.quote: Study AI Agents in theory, design, and practice.

candidates.name: Agent-Builder Dojo
candidates.summary: A short, intensive, small-group practice den (days to weeks) in which builders repeatedly build real agents against real business problems under close facilitation, with measurable output — typically time-to-deploy or hours-saved — as the exit condition.
mechanics: Cohort of 6–20 builders with a shared technical level and real problems from their own org
Daily or weekly build-deploy-review cycles; each cycle ships something runnable
Facilitator unblocks on architecture, prompts, tool plumbing; no lecture-only sessions
Capstone: each participant presents a shipped agent with a measured outcome (e.g. tasks automated, hours freed)
Optional: public leaderboard or peer demo day to surface quality
proof: Each participant ships at least one production-candidate agent by the final session
Aggregate time savings or task-automation count is tracked and reported post-cohort

known_uses.name: Data Science Dojo — Agentic AI Bootcamp
known_uses.quote: 10-week, 30-hour program
known_uses.note: Build and deploy a real multi-agent LLM application as final project; 277+ lessons across 8 courses

known_uses.name: Agilefever Agentic AI BootCamp
known_uses.quote: 7-week program with 52+ hours of live, hands-on training focused on real-world implementations, developing 10+ AI agent solutions
known_uses.note: Capstone required for certificate; 300+ enterprises served

known_uses.name: NEC BluStellar Academy for AI — 20-Day Bootcamp
known_uses.quote: bootcamp program to acquire essential AI skills in 20 days of intensive training
known_uses.note: In-house Japanese program; lang: ja; open course plus company-specific embedding since 2013

known_uses.name: AVILEN AIエージェント研修
known_uses.quote: AIエージェントの仕組みを理解し、基本的なレベルのAIエージェントアプリの構築が可能になること
known_uses.note: Japanese corporate training; two-part: lecture + no-code Dify build; lang: ja

candidates.name: Teach the Failure Modes
candidates.summary: A training session that drills builders on where agents break in production — tool hallucination, instruction drift, infinite loops, non-idempotent replay, metrics blindness — so they design for failure from the start rather than discovering it after deployment.
mechanics: Enumerate the canonical failure modes (tool hallucination, instruction drift, infinite loops, over-eager execution, false success, state desync, flaky tools, non-idempotent replay, permission creep, silent degradation, metrics blindness)
For each mode: show a real production trace or log of the failure happening, not just theory
Builders reproduce a failure in a sandbox and then implement a guard or circuit-breaker for it
Debrief focuses on testing: 'fail loudly in staging instead of silently in production'
Optional: red-team exercise — one team builds an agent, the other tries to break it
proof: Each builder can name at least five failure modes and explain the mitigation
Agent built in accompanying build sprint includes at least two guards (e.g. idempotency check, loop counter, output schema validation)

known_uses.name: Agent Reliability Gap — 12 Early Failure Modes
known_uses.quote: Agents aren't mystical. They're planners + tools + policies wrapped in a loop. Reliability lives in the edges between those boxes.
known_uses.note: Practitioner writeup cataloguing 12 failure modes; widely cited in 2025 enterprise agent deployments

known_uses.name: From Failure Modes to Reliability Awareness in Generative and Agentic AI Systems
known_uses.quote: Awareness is treated not only as a diagnostic score but also as a strategic input for AI governance
known_uses.note: 11-layer failure stack; introduces awareness mapping as a maturity tool; integrates DCAM framework

known_uses.name: Hugging Face AI Agents Course — Bonus Unit 2: Observability and Evaluation
known_uses.quote: You want to ensure that your agents are performing reliably in production.
known_uses.note: Covers tracing, token-cost monitoring, LLM-as-judge, offline benchmarking; part of free cert track

known_uses.name: AIエージェント開発者養成講座実践コース — Trainocate Japan
known_uses.quote: 評価・改善・運用まで含めた一連の進め方を把握し、開発・導入をリードできる
known_uses.note: 3-day Japanese practitioner course; includes tracing, evaluation, and operational failure considerations; lang: ja

sources.title: The Agent Reliability Gap: 12 Early Failure Modes
sources.quote: Most agent failures are boring, not mysterious. They're the same categories we've dealt with for decades — just wrapped in a more flexible planner.

sources.title: From Failure Modes to Reliability Awareness in Generative and Agentic AI System
sources.quote: failures rarely occur in isolation but propagate across layers, creating cascading effects with systemic consequences

candidates.name: Show the Working
candidates.summary: A training module that teaches builders to surface agent reasoning and action traces to end users — via logs, step-by-step summaries, or 'explain-then-act' patterns — so that users can verify agent behaviour and trust the output enough to adopt it.
mechanics: Explain that over 65% of organisations cite 'lack of explainability' as the primary barrier to AI adoption
Demonstrate the 'explain-then-act' pattern: agent outputs reasoning before executing an action
Builders instrument an existing agent with a tracing tool (e.g. OpenTelemetry, Langfuse, LangSmith)
Practise writing human-readable step summaries that a non-technical user can review
Show the three log layers: context (what the agent knew), reasoning (why it chose), actions (what it did)
Optional: add a human-in-the-loop approval gate for high-stakes actions
proof: The agent built by the participant exposes a readable trace or step summary to the end user, not just the final answer
User acceptance test: a non-technical tester can read the trace and confirm whether the agent behaved correctly

known_uses.name: AI Agent Observability — Building Trust Through AI Transparency
known_uses.quote: AI observability is the very foundation of AI trust. It's how you move from hoping your AI agent is behaving and performing correctly to knowing it is.
known_uses.note: Platform vendor; describes activity logs, audit logs, deployment logs as trust infrastructure

known_uses.name: Transparency and Explainability in Agentic AI Decision-Making
known_uses.quote: By exposing the internal logic of agentic decisions, various security teams can validate that agents are operating within their ethical and operational guardrails.
known_uses.note: Describes explain-then-act pattern and three-layer semantic audit trail (context / reasoning / actions)

known_uses.name: Hugging Face AI Agents Course — Bonus Unit 2
known_uses.quote: Monitor Metrics: Track performance indicators such as token usage (costs), latency, and error traces.
known_uses.note: Free curriculum unit; teaches OpenTelemetry instrumentation with smolagents; LLM-as-judge evaluation

known_uses.name: AgentTrace: A Structured Logging Framework for Agent System Observability
known_uses.quote: capturing a rich stream of structured logs across three surfaces: operational, cognitive, and contextual
known_uses.note: 2026 research framework; defines cognitive (reasoning) surface separately from operational (actions)

candidates.name: Platform Agent Certification
candidates.summary: A vendor-issued credential (Salesforce, Microsoft, Google) that certifies a builder can design, configure, and deploy agents on that platform, providing a measurable threshold and a recognised signal for employers.
mechanics: Complete a structured learning path on the vendor platform (Trailhead, Microsoft Learn, Google Skills)
Build one or more agents using the vendor's agent-builder tooling (Agent Builder, Foundry Agent Service, ADK)
Pass a proctored exam or submit a capstone project
Earn a digital badge (Credly or similar) that signals competence at builder level
proof: Passing the vendor exam or completing the capstone earns a named credential (e.g. Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist, Microsoft AI-3026, Google ADK Technical Expert Badge)

known_uses.name: Salesforce Certified Agentforce Specialist
known_uses.quote: Certified Agentforce Specialists are responsible for managing and optimizing Agentforce and have deep understanding of both Salesforce platform configuration and Agentforce capabilities.
known_uses.note: Formerly AI Specialist; free first attempt until Dec 2025; requires Agentblazer Trailhead path

known_uses.name: Develop AI Agents on Azure (AI-3026)
known_uses.quote: By the end of this learning path, you will have the skills needed to develop AI agents on Azure.
known_uses.note: 9-module learning path; covers Foundry Agent Service, custom tools, MCP, multi-agent orchestration, A2A protocol; free on Microsoft Learn

known_uses.name: Agentic AI on Google Cloud
known_uses.quote: accelerate knowledge exchange, integrate with enterprise databases, and deploy secure, advanced AI agent solutions on Google Cloud
known_uses.note: 9 activities; covers Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform (ex-Vertex AI), ADK, multi-agent security; technical expert badge

candidates.name: Framework Deep-Dive
candidates.summary: A focused course on a single agent-orchestration framework (LangGraph, CrewAI, smolagents, AutoGen/AG2) that takes a builder beyond hello-world into state management, memory, human-in-the-loop, streaming, and production deployment.
mechanics: Module 1: core graph/state model of the chosen framework
Module 2: memory and persistence (short-term vs long-term)
Module 3: human-in-the-loop hooks and streaming
Module 4: multi-agent orchestration within the framework
Module 5: deployment and monitoring
Each module ends with a runnable project students can extend
proof: Participant deploys a stateful, multi-turn agent with at least one tool integration and one human-in-the-loop checkpoint

known_uses.name: Foundation: Introduction to LangGraph — LangChain Academy
known_uses.quote: LangGraph helps developers add better precision and control into agentic workflows.
known_uses.note: Free; 55 lessons, 6 hours; builds agents with memory, streaming, human-in-the-loop, long-term memory, deployment

known_uses.name: Project: Deep Agents with LangGraph — LangChain Academy
known_uses.quote: shipping reliable agents faster
known_uses.note: Free; 30 lessons, 2 hours; builds subagents, file-handling agents, production-ready compositions

known_uses.note: 3-day Japanese practitioner course; LangChain, LangGraph, Deep Agents; includes tracing and RAG; lang: ja

candidates.name: Center-of-Excellence AI-Native Engineering
candidates.summary: An enterprise Center of Excellence dedicated to training internal builders to design, build, and run agents on the company's own stack, with vendor technical-enablement support, so the organisation develops a standing agent-building capacity rather than one-off experiments.
mechanics: Stand up a dedicated CoE team (typically 5–20 engineers) with a mandate to build production agents
Vendor (or specialist consultancy) provides technical enablement: architecture guidance, code review, failure-mode reviews
CoE runs a rolling cohort programme: new builders embed with the CoE for 4–12 weeks, build a real agent, then return to their team as a local agent-building expert
CoE publishes internal reference architectures, prompt libraries, and evaluation harnesses
Exit criterion: CoE-trained engineers ship agents with measurable business outcomes (time saved, tasks automated)
proof: CoE cohort graduates who each own a shipped, monitored agent in production
Internal reference architecture adopted by teams outside the CoE

known_uses.name: NEC / Anthropic AI-Native Engineering CoE
known_uses.quote: NEC will establish an internal Center of Excellence (CoE) with the aim of developing highly skilled AI professionals, utilizing technical support and training provided by Anthropic.
known_uses.note: 30,000 NEC employees on Claude globally; CoE targets one of Japan's largest AI-native engineering teams; announced April 2026

known_uses.name: NTT DATA OpenAI Center of Excellence
known_uses.quote: NTT DATA Launches Smart AI Agent™ to Accelerate Generative AI Adoption
known_uses.note: CoE serves as knowledge hub for OpenAI-stack agent implementation; targets $2B agent revenue by 2027; tens of thousands of GenAI practitioners
